question: Could not extract presale address from receipt
answer: This usually means deploy receipt parsing failed. Verify both wallet confirmations were completed, wait for final confirmation on-chain, check explorer logs for presale creation event, refresh My Launches, and retry. If still failing, share tx hash, token address, and wallet with support.

question: How many confirmations are required when deploying a presale
answer: Two confirmations are required. First you approve token transfer. Second the factory deploys the presale and pulls the required token amount.

question: what causes deploy failure during presale creation
answer: Common causes are wrong chain, insufficient gas, rejected wallet confirmation, incomplete token approval, or invalid config outside platform limits.

question: what should i check before pressing deploy presale
answer: Confirm token balance, rates, caps, min/max buy, liquidity and lock settings, timeline, vesting config, and project links. Keep enough native token for gas and listing fee.

question: wen launch bro
answer: Check exact start time and timezone on the sale countdown. If time passed and buy is still locked, verify chain, whitelist rules, and sale status.

question: how do i buy into dis presale
answer: Open the sale page, connect wallet on the correct chain, enter an amount within min/max limits, then confirm transaction. Claim after successful finalization.

question: i conected my wallet but cant see the buy buton
answer: If Buy is missing, check correct network, Live status, whitelist requirement, and wallet eligibility. Refresh and reconnect wallet if needed.

question: is this legit or another rug
answer: MoonSale has contract-level protections like refund and lock logic, but project-level risk still exists. Always review team credibility, token distribution, lock terms, and community quality before investing.

question: which chain dis on eth or bnb
answer: MoonSale supports BNB Chain and Ethereum. Each sale runs on one chain, shown on the sale page.

question: do i need bnb or eth to participte
answer: Use native coin of the sale chain: BNB for BNB Chain sales and ETH for Ethereum sales.

question: bro how much minimum i can invest
answer: Minimum contribution is set per sale and enforced on-chain. Check the sale panel for exact min buy.

question: wats the max i can put in one presale
answer: Maximum contribution is set per sale. Your wallet cannot exceed that configured max amount.

question: i sent bnb but tokens didnt come yet
answer: Contributed funds are recorded first. Tokens become claimable only after successful finalization and according to vesting terms.

question: bro i clicked buy and metamask showed error wht do i do
answer: Check correct chain, available gas, and min/max limits. Retry transaction and inspect explorer error reason. If still failing, share tx hash with support.

question: can i invest 2 times in same presale or only once
answer: Usually yes, multiple contributions are allowed while sale is Live, as long as your total stays within max limit.

question: how do i kno how many tokens ill get before buying
answer: Use the contribution estimate on the sale page. In fixed-rate presales it is stable by rate; in fair launch it is an estimate that can change until finalization.

question: wen can i claim my tokens
answer: Claim opens after successful finalization. If vesting exists, only currently unlocked portion is claimable.

question: sale ended 2 hrs ago still no claim button
answer: Claim appears only after finalize action is completed. If not finalized yet, claim stays unavailable.

question: do i need to do anythng or tokens come automaticly
answer: You usually need to click Claim from the sale page. Tokens are not automatically sent in most sale flows.

question: i claimed but tokens not in my wallet
answer: Verify claim tx success, correct chain, and token contract import in your wallet. Also make sure you are using the same contribution wallet.

question: where do i add the token to my metamask after claim
answer: In MetaMask use Import Tokens and paste the token contract address from the official sale page.

question: project finalized but i still cant claim
answer: Refresh page, reconnect same wallet, confirm correct network, and check vesting/cliff settings. If still blocked, contact admin with sale link and wallet address.

question: softcap not reached wen do i get my money back
answer: If softcap is not reached, sale fails and refund is available via smart contract on the sale page.

question: how do i get refund if project fails
answer: Open the failed or cancelled sale page and click Refund using the same wallet that contributed.

question: bro i want out before sale ends can i withdraw
answer: If early withdrawal is enabled, you can exit during Live phase, usually with a penalty.

question: if i withdraw early do i lose all my bnb or just some
answer: Early withdrawal usually applies a penalty, not total loss. Exact amount depends on sale rules.

question: sale got cancled by dev will i get full refund
answer: If sale is cancelled and refund is enabled by contract conditions, contributors can refund from the sale page.

question: is refund automatic or do i have to click something
answer: You usually need to click Refund. Eligibility is on-chain, but claiming refund still requires wallet transaction.

question: wats vesting mean in simple words
answer: Vesting means your tokens unlock gradually over time instead of all at once.

question: i got 20 percent on tge wen do i get the rest
answer: Remaining tokens unlock according to cliff and vesting duration configured for that sale.

question: bro cliff means what exactly
answer: Cliff is the waiting period before additional vesting unlocks begin.

question: how do i check if lp is actually locked
answer: Use official lock references from the sale and verify lock contract data on chain.

question: team tokens locked or not how do i verify
answer: Check team allocation lock or vesting contract addresses provided by project and verify on explorer.

question: vesting schedule can dev cancel it or not
answer: That depends on vesting contract design. Review contract behavior and sale terms before investing.

question: is moonsale safe or can devs rug anytime
answer: MoonSale has audited contracts and investor safeguards, but project risk remains. Always do your own research before investing.

question: how is dis diffrent from pinksale
answer: Use official docs to compare launch flow, fees, refund behavior, and lock rules. I can provide verified MoonSale details for side-by-side checks.

question: bro the dev dmd me saying send bnb for whitelist is that real
answer: Do not send funds through DMs. Only contribute via official MoonSale sale pages.

question: kyc badge means dev is doxxed
answer: KYC badge indicates identity verification was completed under platform process. It does not remove all risk.

question: audit badge means contract is safe
answer: Audit badge is a positive signal, not a guarantee. Market and team risks can still exist.

question: can moonsale team steal my funds
answer: Sale and refund logic are contract-enforced on chain, not manual admin custody.

question: no kyc on this project shud i still invest
answer: No KYC means higher uncertainty. Review risk factors carefully and only invest what you can afford to lose.

question: who audited moonsale itself
answer: MoonSale states its core contracts were independently audited before mainnet deployment. Use official docs for current audit references.

question: wrong network showing wht do i do
answer: Switch wallet to the chain used by the sale, then refresh and reconnect.

question: i approved but 2nd txn failed now what
answer: Approval can succeed while buy fails due to gas, limits, or wrong chain. Recheck settings and retry.

question: gas fee too high any way to reduce
answer: Wait for lower congestion or tune gas settings carefully. Very low gas can cause failure or long pending transactions.

question: bro transaction pending for 20min is it stuck
answer: Pending often means congestion or low gas. Check explorer and use speed-up or cancel in wallet if available.

question: i used wrong wallet address can i fix it
answer: On-chain contribution ownership cannot be reassigned by support. Actions must come from original wallet.

question: metamask keeps rejecting the txn idk why
answer: Check chain, gas balance, pending queue, and contribution limits. Retry after fixing those conditions.

question: wats fair launch mean vs normal presale
answer: Presale has fixed token rate and hardcap. Fair launch uses dynamic price discovery from total raised and token pool.

question: price keeps changing on fair launch is that normal
answer: Yes. Fair launch estimates can change until finalization as total raised changes.

question: if more ppl join does my token amount go down
answer: In fair launch your share is proportional. If total raised grows and you do not add more, your percentage share decreases.

question: wen is final price decided
answer: Final fair launch price is set at finalization using total raised and token pool.

question: bro i joined fair launch early will i get better price or same as everyone
answer: In standard fair launch, contributors settle with the same final pricing formula. Early entry does not guarantee a separate fixed tier.

question: how do i kno if im whitelisted
answer: Whitelist status is checked by contract when you contribute. Non-approved wallets are rejected in whitelist-only sales.

question: im not on whitelist can i still buy
answer: Only if sale is public. In whitelist-only mode, non-whitelisted wallets cannot buy.

question: whitelist full can i still apply
answer: Whitelist changes are controlled by project team. Ask official project channels for updates.

question: dev said whitelist open how do i join
answer: Follow official project channels and never send funds to private wallets for whitelist access.

question: i got whitelisted but sale starts wen
answer: Check the sale countdown for exact start time and timezone.

question: ok so i connect wallet then what
answer: Open a Live sale, review limits and tokenomics, enter amount, confirm tx, and claim after finalization.

question: where do i even find presales on this site

question: do i need to make an account or just wallet
answer: Wallet connection is usually enough for on-chain participation.

question: is moonsale an exchange or what exactly

question: where do i see all my investments in one place
answer: Use My Contributions or invested views to track joined sales, claim state, and refund status.

question: can i use moonsale on my phone
answer: Yes, MoonSale can be used on mobile with compatible wallet browser or WalletConnect flow.

question: bro can u send me step by step how to invest
answer: 1) Open sale page. 2) Connect wallet on correct chain. 3) Enter amount within limits. 4) Confirm tx. 5) Claim after finalization.

question: i dont hav metamask can i use trust wallet

question: can i use coinbase wallet
answer: If it supports required chain and connection flow, yes.

question: can i use hardware wallet like ledger

question: can i contribute from exchange wallet like binance directly
answer: Not recommended. Use a self-custody wallet you control for contributions and claims.

question: i changed wallet mid presale will my old contribution be lost
answer: Contribution remains tied to original wallet. Claim and refund must be done from that wallet.

question: bro price in presale is different from price after listing why
answer: Presale and listing prices can differ based on launch setup and liquidity configuration.

question: my estimated tokens keep changing on fair launch is that a bug
answer: Usually not a bug. Fair launch estimates update with changing total raised before finalization.

question: hardcap reached in 2 min i didnt get in is there another chance
answer: If hardcap is filled, further buys are blocked. Track upcoming launches for next opportunity.

question: bro i have usdt can i use that or only bnb eth
answer: Contribution asset depends on sale design. Most native-chain launches use BNB on BNB Chain or ETH on Ethereum.

question: if i lose access to my wallet do i lose vested tokens forever
answer: Yes. On-chain claims require the original wallet access. Keep seed phrase and keys secure.

question: vesting says claimable 0 but cliff should be done by now

question: how often can i claim during vesting period like daily or only once
answer: Claim cadence depends on vesting schedule. Some are continuous-linear, others interval-based.

question: dev hasnt finalized its been 2 days since sale ended should i be worried
answer: Finalization must happen for claim flow to open. If delayed, contact project/admin support and keep transaction records.

question: sale finalized but claim button still not showing its been 3 hours
answer: Recheck wallet and chain, refresh page, then verify vesting conditions. If still missing, contact admin with sale link and wallet.

question: sale failed but refund button not showing
answer: Use exact contribution wallet on correct chain. If still hidden, send sale link and wallet to admin support.

question: i got partial refund where did rest go
answer: Partial return can happen with early-withdraw penalties or mixed transaction states. Verify each tx on explorer.

question: failed sale refund has deadline or can i claim anytime
answer: MoonSale documentation states no refund deadline for failed or cancelled sales.

question: if i contributed in 3 separate transactions do i refund all at once

answer: No. Whitelist and contribution are sale and chain specific. BSC and ETH launches are separate contracts.

question: fair launch has no hardcap so it can go forever
answer: No. Fair launch has defined start/end time even without hardcap.

question: is there slippage in fair launch like on dex

question: can dev cancel fair launch after it starts
answer: Cancellation behavior depends on contract conditions and current sale state.

question: difference between kyc badge and audit badge

question: whats the moonsale buybot do exactly
answer: MoonSale BuyBot posts real-time buy updates in Telegram for supported tokens.

question: how do i add buybot to my telegram group
answer: Use official MoonSale BuyBot instructions and grant required Telegram bot permissions in your group.

question: token scanner says honeypot but others say its fine which to trust

question: tokenomics creator is free or paid

question: can i use token generator without launching presale

question: how do i lock lp tokens that i already have

question: token management page shows nothing why
answer: Connect the wallet with ownership/permissions and ensure correct chain selection.

question: platform takes percent of raised funds how much exactly

question: listing fee is per chain or same everywhere
answer: Fee policy can vary by chain/product. Verify exact current values on official fees page.

question: if presale fails do i get listing fee back
answer: Listing-fee refund depends on current platform policy. Check official docs for latest terms.

question: bro total fees to launch a presale roughly how much in bnb

question: wen moon

question: i cant read all this just tell me buy or no buy

question: anon dev instant red flag or nah

question: someone in tg said moonsale is hacked is that true
answer: Ignore unverified rumors. Trust only official MoonSale announcements and status updates.

question: can i extend liquidity lock now my presale is running and in live mode
answer: Once a presale or fair launch contract is deployed and Live, liquidity lock cannot be extended during live mode. Extend only after finalization.

question: can i extend liquidity lock during active fair launch
answer: No. During active live mode lock extension is not available. Finalize first, then adjust lock if supported.

question: its past start time but i cant buy yet why
answer: Check whitelist mode, chain mismatch, and whether publish/final setup steps are completed.

question: sale supposed to end today but still showing live
answer: Confirm timezone/block-time interpretation. UI cache can temporarily lag status updates.

question: wen does my next vesting unlock happen
answer: Next unlock is determined by vesting start, cliff, and interval schedule in contract.

question: help my bnb is gone and no tokens
answer: Verify tx hash and contribution record on sale page. Contributions do not instantly appear as tokens before finalization. If mismatch remains, contact admin with wallet and tx hash.

question: if you do not know the answer what should happen
answer: If no verified answer is available, ask user to wait for admin support reply and share official docs links as temporary guidance.

question: what does token failed eligibility check mean in fair launch
answer: It means the eligibility scanner found a blocking condition that must be fixed before deployment.

question: why was this blocked dex pair reserves pair has existing liquidity
answer: This token already has liquidity in an existing DEX pair. Launching another initial pool would create extraction risk, so deployment is blocked.

question: pair has existing liquidity why dangerous
answer: Existing holders can arbitrage or drain value from a newly added pool if the launch starts with an already-liquid pair.

question: how to fix pair has existing liquidity block
answer: Remove existing pair liquidity first, then re-run eligibility scan, or launch with a token that has no existing liquid pair.

question: can i launch fair launch if dex pair already has liquidity
answer: No, not until that existing liquidity condition is resolved.

question: can i bypass token eligibility scan
answer: No. Blocking eligibility checks must pass before deployment.

question: what does token ownership check amount mean
answer: It shows your wallet token balance available for deposit and confirms ownership is detectable.

question: proxy detection original contract meaning
answer: Original contract means proxy behavior was not detected in that scan result.

question: honeypot detection testnet not applicable meaning
answer: That check is not available in current testnet context, so result is informational rather than pass or fail.

question: transfer fee check testnet not applicable meaning
answer: Transfer-tax behavior could not be reliably assessed in testnet mode for that scan.

question: blacklist detection no blacklist found meaning
answer: No blacklist behavior was detected in the scan context.

question: why scanner shows ticks and arrows together
answer: Ticks indicate passed checks; arrow-style indicators often show informational or not-applicable checks rather than hard failure.

question: what does change token button do in fair launch creator
answer: It exits current token context so you can select a different token and rerun eligibility flow.

question: what does draft saved start over mean
answer: Draft data is stored temporarily, and Start over resets the form flow so you can rebuild settings.

question: fair launch steps token config info deploy
answer: Flow is Token selection, Config setup, Project Info, then Review and Deploy.

question: project name from token can i edit
answer: Project name is auto-filled from token metadata in this flow. If token metadata is wrong, update token source details or change token.

question: is logo url optional in project info
answer: Yes, logo URL is optional but recommended for trust and presentation.

question: description field max length in fair launch info
answer: The description field in that form supports up to 1500 characters.

question: is whitepaper url optional in fair launch info
answer: Yes, whitepaper URL is optional in that project info form.

question: which project info links should i fill
answer: Website, X, Telegram, and optional GitHub, Discord, and whitepaper should be filled with official links for better credibility.

question: can i continue fair launch with optional fields empty
answer: Yes for optional fields, but complete and verified project info is strongly recommended.

question: tokens for sale vs tokens for liquidity difference in fair launch
answer: Tokens for Sale are allocated to contributors; Tokens for Liquidity are reserved for DEX pool creation at finalization.

question: how is total deposit calculated in review
answer: Total Deposit equals Tokens for Sale plus Tokens for Liquidity.

question: why total deposit is less than full supply
answer: Because a small amount remains unlocked in wallet by design, as long as it stays within platform unlocked limit.

question: softcap 0.01 bnb meaning in fair launch
answer: Softcap is the minimum raise threshold required for a successful finalization.

question: min buy 0.01 bnb meaning
answer: Each contribution must be at least 0.01 BNB in that configuration.

question: liquidity 65 percent locked 90d meaning
answer: 65% of raised funds is used for liquidity and LP lock duration is set to 90 days.

question: start utc and end utc in deploy review meaning
answer: These are sale open and close times in UTC timezone.

question: whitelist open to everyone meaning
answer: Sale is public, so any eligible wallet on correct chain can contribute.

question: price at softcap shows 0.00000000 bnb per token why
answer: That usually means displayed precision is rounding a very small number. The underlying fair-launch math still applies.

question: tokens to deposit value in review meaning
answer: It is the exact token amount your wallet must approve and transfer to deploy the fair launch contract.

question: platform fee 2 percent of successful raise when charged
answer: This fee is applied only on successful raise outcome, typically at finalization stage.

question: is 2 percent platform fee charged if fair launch fails
answer: The label says fee is on successful raise, so failed outcome does not trigger that success-based fee.

question: sufficient token balance check meaning in deploy review
answer: It confirms your wallet has at least the exact required token amount for contract deposit.

question: unlocked tokens within limit meaning in fair launch
answer: After deposit, remaining wallet tokens must stay below platform max unlocked threshold.

question: platform unlocked token limit 20 percent meaning
answer: Creator cannot leave more than 20% of total supply unlocked after deployment.

question: after depositing 100000 point 9 remains unlocked what does this show
answer: It shows post-deploy remaining wallet balance and its supply percentage used for limit enforcement.

question: can i set unlocked tokens above platform limit
answer: No. Deployment is blocked if post-deposit unlocked amount exceeds platform threshold.

question: what happens after deploy fair launch
answer: Tokens are held in contract, investors contribute until end time, creator finalizes if softcap is met, liquidity is added at fair price, and investors claim instantly for no-vesting setup.

question: who finalizes fair launch after softcap
answer: Creator finalizes after sale end when softcap condition is met.

question: when do investors claim tokens in no vesting fair launch
answer: After successful finalization, claims are available instantly in no-vesting configuration.

question: no vesting in deploy review meaning
answer: No vesting means contributors receive fully claimable allocation at claim-open, not gradual unlock.

question: market sets token price in fair launch meaning
answer: Final token price is discovered from total raised amount and total token pool at finalization.

question: deposit your entire token pool upfront meaning
answer: All launch tokens required for sale plus liquidity are deposited to contract before launch starts.

question: if softcap is not met in fair launch what happens
answer: Sale is unsuccessful and contributors use refund flow according to contract rules.

question: can i edit fair launch config after deploy
answer: No, deploy creates immutable on-chain sale config. Reconfigure before deploy if changes are needed.

question: if i remove existing pair liquidity can i retry fair launch
answer: Yes, after resolving existing-liquidity condition, re-run scan and proceed if all blocking checks pass.

question: pair exists but empty vs pair has existing liquidity difference
answer: Pair exists but empty has zero reserves and may be acceptable; pair has existing liquidity means non-zero reserves and can trigger block.

question: testnet not applicable checks should i worry
answer: Not-applicable checks are expected on some testnets; still ensure all mandatory blocking checks pass.

question: scanner passed ownership but still blocked why
answer: Any single blocking condition can fail deployment even if other checks pass.

question: can i use a fresh token without existing pair to pass eligibility
answer: Yes, a token without an already-liquid pair avoids that specific DEX-reserve blocking condition.

question: where can i review all fair launch values before deploy
answer: Use the Review and Deploy step to verify token amounts, caps, liquidity, timing, whitelist mode, and fee notes before confirming.

question: should i convert local time to utc before setting fair launch
answer: Yes. The launch form uses UTC, so convert local timezone carefully to avoid wrong start or end window.

question: if i click start over does fair launch draft get erased
answer: Start over resets builder flow. If draft persistence exists, always re-check every field before deploy.

question: if i change token after filling info will values reset
answer: Changing token can alter dependent values. Re-check config, info, and deposit calculations before deploying.
